questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Four elastic cords each of force constant K and almost zero relaxed lengths are connected at their one end to a particle P of mass m and the other ends are connected to diametrically opposite points of two perpendicular diameters AC, BD. Mass of the ring is uniformly distributed and is 2 m . In equilibrium the system stays at rest with the particle at the center of the ring $\mathrm{O}^2$. $\mathrm{O}^{\prime}$ coincides at this instant with origin ' O ' of xx-plane (Initial orientation of OA and OB is x and y axes) in which ring is present. Now the ring is held at rest and the particle is pulled in xy plane along a direction $37^{\circ}$ to x axis (along dotted line shown in figure) by a small distance $r_0$ and then both ring and particle are released at $\mathrm{t}=0$.
Question: Assuming initial location of the center of the ring to be origin and the initial displacement $r_0$ to be along $+\mathbf{X}$ axis (different from x axis) the position of center of ring $\mathrm{X}_i$ with respect to time t is given by (Assume angular frequency of oscillation is $\omega$ )
questions.solution:
For keq
So Net restoring force along Displace went $\begin{aligned} & =2 k r_0 \cos 37^{\circ} \cdot \cos 33^{\circ}+e^k r_0 \cos 53^{\circ} \cos 3^{\circ} \\ & =2 k r_0\left(\left(\frac{4}{3}\right)^2+\left(\frac{3}{5}\right)^2\right) \\ & =2 k r_0 \end{aligned}$ Reduce mass of system $=\frac{2 m \cdot m}{3 m}=\frac{2^m}{3}$ so Time period $=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{m}{3 k}}$
Amplitude of motion of center of ring $=\frac{r_0}{3}$ at $t=0$ centre of ring is at exteome position so $\quad x_r=\frac{\pi}{3}(1-\cos \omega t)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: The time-period of a simple pendulum performing simple harmonic motion, having length $\ell$ is given $\mathrm{T}=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{\ell}{\mathrm{~g}}}$ This formula holds good if following conditions are satisfied. (i) String is massless and extensible. (ii) Bob is a point mass. (iii) Angular displacement about mean position is very small, and (iv) Length of string is very small as compared to the radius of earth $\left(\mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{e}}\right)$.Question: If the bob is a solid sphere of radius $R$ and length of string is $L$, then equivalent length of pendulum will be
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{R}=$ radius of gyration about mass centre $=\sqrt{\frac{2 \mathrm{R}}{5}}$
$\ell=$ distance between point of suspension and mass centre $=\mathrm{L}+\mathrm{R}$
$\ell_{\mathrm{eq}}=\frac{\mathrm{k}^2}{\ell}+\ell=\frac{2}{5} \frac{\mathrm{R}^2}{(\mathrm{~L}+\mathrm{R})}+(\mathrm{L}+\mathrm{R})$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: The time-period of a simple pendulum performing simple harmonic motion, having length $\ell$ is given $\mathrm{T}=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{\ell}{\mathrm{~g}}}$ This formula holds good if following conditions are satisfied. (i) String is massless and extensible. (ii) Bob is a point mass. (iii) Angular displacement about mean position is very small, and (iv) Length of string is very small as compared to the radius of earth $\left(\mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{e}}\right)$.Question: If the bob is a hollow spherical shell of radius $R$ and length string is $L$. The time period of pendulum is $T_1$. Now bob is filled with water completes and now its time period become $T_2$ then $T_1 / T_2$ is
questions.solution:
When the bob is hollow $\ell_{\text {eq }}=\frac{2 \mathrm{R}^2}{3(\ell+\mathrm{R})}+(\mathrm{L}+\mathrm{R}) \Rightarrow \mathrm{T}_1=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{\ell_{\mathrm{eq}}}{\mathrm{g}}}=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{\frac{2 \mathrm{R}^2}{3(\mathrm{~L}+\mathrm{R})}+(\mathrm{L}+\mathrm{R})}{\mathrm{g}}}$
when filled with water, total energy
$\begin{aligned}
& E=\frac{1}{2} m_w \omega^2(L+R)^2+\frac{1}{2} M \omega^2(L+R)^2+\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{2}{3} M \omega^2 R^2+\left(M_w+M\right) g(L+R)(1-\cos \theta) \\
& \frac{d E}{d t}=0 \Rightarrow \frac{d^2 \theta}{d t^2}+\left[\frac{g(L+R)}{\frac{2}{3} \frac{M R^2}{\left(M+M_w\right)}+(L+R)^2}\right] \theta=0 \Rightarrow T_2=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{\frac{2}{3}\left(\frac{M}{M+M_w}\right) \frac{R^2}{L+R}+(L+R)}{g}}
\end{aligned}$
Therefore $\mathrm{T}_2 \lt \mathrm{T}_1 \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{~T}_1}{\mathrm{~T}_2}\gt1$
$OR$
Water will not rotate therefore system has more translation KE, hence more average speed for same amplitude. So time period will decrease.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: A cube made of wood having specific gravity 0.4 and side length a is floated in a large tank full of water.Question: If the cube is depressed slightly, it executes SHM from it's position. What is it's time period?

questions.q:
Paragraph: Four elastic cords each of force constant K and almost zero relaxed lengths are connected at their one end to a particle P of mass m and the other ends are connected to diametrically opposite points of two perpendicular diameters AC, BD. Mass of the ring is uniformly distributed and is 2 m . In equilibrium the system stays at rest with the particle at the center of the ring $\mathrm{O}^2$. $\mathrm{O}^{\prime}$ coincides at this instant with origin ' O ' of xx-plane (Initial orientation of OA and OB is x and y axes) in which ring is present. Now the ring is held at rest and the particle is pulled in xy plane along a direction $37^{\circ}$ to x axis (along dotted line shown in figure) by a small distance $r_0$ and then both ring and particle are released at $\mathrm{t}=0$.
Question: The time period of small oscillations of the system?
questions.solution:
For keq
So Net restoring force along Displace went $\begin{aligned} & =2 k r_0 \cos 37^{\circ} \cdot \cos 33^{\circ}+e^k r_0 \cos 53^{\circ} \cos 3^{\circ} \\ & =2 k r_0\left(\left(\frac{4}{3}\right)^2+\left(\frac{3}{5}\right)^2\right) \\ & =2 k r_0 \end{aligned}$ Reduce mass of system $=\frac{2 m \cdot m}{3 m}=\frac{2^m}{3}$ so Time period $=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{m}{3 k}}$
Amplitude of motion of center of ring $=\frac{r_0}{3}$ at $t=0$ centre of ring is at exteome position so $\quad x_r=\frac{\pi}{3}(1-\cos \omega t)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: The time-period of a simple pendulum performing simple harmonic motion, having length $\ell$ is given $\mathrm{T}=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{\ell}{\mathrm{~g}}}$ This formula holds good if following conditions are satisfied. (i) String is massless and extensible. (ii) Bob is a point mass. (iii) Angular displacement about mean position is very small, and (iv) Length of string is very small as compared to the radius of earth $\left(\mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{e}}\right)$.Question: Now the water of bob is completely freezes into ice and this phenomena does not produce any change in $L$. The new time period becomes $T_3$ then $T_2 / T_3$ is
questions.solution:
When water freezes it rotates $\mathrm{T}_3=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{\frac{2 \mathrm{R}^2}{5(\mathrm{~L}+\mathrm{R})}+(\mathrm{L}+\mathrm{R})}{\mathrm{g}}} \Rightarrow \mathrm{T}_3 \lt \mathrm{T}_2$
OR
Ice will rotate therefore system has less translation KE, hence for same amplitude average speed is less. So time period will increase.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q:
Paragraph: Figure shows block A of mass 0.2 kg sliding to the right over a frictionless elevated surface at a speed of $10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. The block undergoes a collision with stationary block $B$, which is connected to a non-deformed spring of spring constant $1000 \mathrm{Nm}^{-1}$. The coefficient of restitution between the blocks is 0.5. After the collision, block B oscillates in SHM with a period of 0.2 s, and block A slides off the opposite end of the elevated surface, landing a distance ' d ' from the base of that surface after falling height 5 m. (use $\pi^2=10 ; \mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ ) Assume that the spring does not affect the collision.
Question: Mass of the block B is-
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Figure shows block A of mass 0.2 kg sliding to the right over a frictionless elevated surface at a speed of $10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. The block undergoes a collision with stationary block $B$, which is connected to a non-deformed spring of spring constant $1000 \mathrm{Nm}^{-1}$. The coefficient of restitution between the blocks is 0.5 . After the collision, block B oscillates in SHM with a period of 0.2 s , and block A slides off the opposite end of the elevated surface, landing a distance ' d ' from the base of that surface after falling height 5 m . (use $\pi^2=10 ; \mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ ) Assume that the spring does not affect the collision.
Question: Amplitude of the SHM as being executed by block B-spring system is-
questions.solution:
Immediately after the collision, suppose velocities of the blocks are $v_1$ and $v_2$ as shown $\frac{1}{2}$ (velocity of approach) $=$ velocity of separation. $\Rightarrow 5=v_2-v_1 \ldots$ (i)
Using principal of conservation of momentum for the collision $2=0.2 \mathrm{v}_1+\mathrm{v}_2 \Rightarrow 10=\mathrm{v}_1+5 \mathrm{v}_2 \ldots$ (ii) On solving $\mathrm{v}_2=2.5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ and $\mathrm{v}_1=-2.5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. Hence block A moves leftward after the collision with speed $2.5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. And the block B moves towards right with speed $2.5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. The maximum velocity of $B=2.5=\omega \mathrm{A} \Rightarrow \mathrm{A}=\mathrm{v} \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{k}}}=2.5 \sqrt{\frac{1}{1000}} \mathrm{~m}=2.5 \sqrt{10} \mathrm{~cm}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics

questions.q:
Paragraph: Figure shows block A of mass 0.2 kg sliding to the right over a frictionless elevated surface at a speed of $10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. The block undergoes a collision with stationary block $B$, which is connected to a non-deformed spring of spring constant $1000 \mathrm{Nm}^{-1}$. The coefficient of restitution between the blocks is 0.5. After the collision, block B oscillates in SHM with a period of 0.2 s, and block A slides off the opposite end of the elevated surface, landing a distance ' d ' from the base of that surface after falling height 5 m. (use $\pi^2=10 ; \mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ ) Assume that the spring does not affect the collision.
Question: The distance 'd' will be equal to-
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: A horizontal spring block system executes SHM with amplitude A $=10 \mathrm{~cm}$ initial phase $\phi=0$ and angular frequency $\omega$. The mass of block is $M=13 \mathrm{~kg}$ and there is no friction between the block and the horizontal surface. The spring constant being $2500 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}$. At $\mathrm{t}=\mathrm{t}_1 \sec$ [for which $\omega \mathrm{t}_1=\phi_1=30^{\circ}$ ]. A mass $\mathrm{m}=12 \mathrm{~kg}$ is gently put on the block. [Assume that collision between the block and the mass is perfectly inelastic and mass m remains stationary w.r.t. the block M always]
Question: The total energy of system after collision at any moment of time is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \omega=\sqrt{\frac{k}{M}}, \omega^{\prime}=\sqrt{\frac{k}{M+m}}=\sqrt{\frac{2500}{25}}=10 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{sec} \\ & \omega^{\prime}=\omega \sqrt{\frac{M}{M+m}} \\ & x_1=A \sin \phi_1\end{aligned}$
Potential energy stored in the spring at $\left(t=t_1\right)=\frac{1}{2} k x_1^2=\frac{1}{2} M \omega^2 \sin ^2 \phi_1$
Let v and v ' be the velocity of system just before collision and just after collision, so using COLM
$v^{\prime}=\frac{M v}{(M+m)}=\frac{M A \omega \cos \theta}{(M+m)}$
Total energy after collision $=\mathrm{PE}+\mathrm{KE}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{kx}_1^2+\frac{1}{2}(\mathrm{M}+\mathrm{m}) \mathrm{v}^{\prime 2}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{1}{2} M A^2 \omega^2\left[\frac{M+m \sin ^2 \theta}{M+m}\right]=\frac{1}{2} k A^2\left[\frac{M+m \sin ^2 \theta}{M+m}\right]=8 J \\ & \frac{1}{2}(M+m) A^{\prime 2} \omega^{\prime 2}=\frac{1}{2} M A^2 \omega^2\left[\frac{M+m \sin ^2 \theta}{M+m}\right]\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: A horizontal spring block system executes SHM with amplitude A $=10 \mathrm{~cm}$ initial phase $\phi=0$ and angular frequency $\omega$. The mass of block is $M=13 \mathrm{~kg}$ and there is no friction between the block and the horizontal surface. The spring constant being $2500 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}$. At $\mathrm{t}=\mathrm{t}_1 \sec$ [for which $\omega \mathrm{t}_1=\phi_1=30^{\circ}$ ]. A mass $\mathrm{m}=12 \mathrm{~kg}$ is gently put on the block. [Assume that collision between the block and the mass is perfectly inelastic and mass m remains stationary w.r.t. the block M always]
Question: The new angular frequency of the system will be
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \omega=\sqrt{\frac{k}{M}}, \omega^{\prime}=\sqrt{\frac{k}{M+m}}=\sqrt{\frac{2500}{25}}=10 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{sec} \\ & \omega^{\prime}=\omega \sqrt{\frac{M}{M+m}} \\ & x_1=A \sin \phi_1\end{aligned}$
Potential energy stored in the spring at $\left(t=t_1\right)=\frac{1}{2} k x_1^2=\frac{1}{2} M \omega^2 \sin ^2 \phi_1$
Let v and v ' be the velocity of system just before collision and just after collision, so using COLM
$v^{\prime}=\frac{M v}{(M+m)}=\frac{M A \omega \cos \theta}{(M+m)}$
Total energy after collision $=\mathrm{PE}+\mathrm{KE}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{kx}_1^2+\frac{1}{2}(\mathrm{M}+\mathrm{m}) \mathrm{v}^{\prime 2}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{1}{2} M A^2 \omega^2\left[\frac{M+m \sin ^2 \theta}{M+m}\right]=\frac{1}{2} k A^2\left[\frac{M+m \sin ^2 \theta}{M+m}\right]=8 J \\ & \frac{1}{2}(M+m) A^{\prime 2} \omega^{\prime 2}=\frac{1}{2} M A^2 \omega^2\left[\frac{M+m \sin ^2 \theta}{M+m}\right]\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
